channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia fires ~800 drones at Ukraine in massive barrage despite peace talks
item.description: Russia launched one of the wars largest drone attacks, killing at least six people, as Putin and Trump had spoken of possible peace. The barrage targeted 20 regions across Ukraine.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Netanyahu made secret visit to UAE during Iran war
item.description: Israeli PM Netanyahu secretly visited the UAE to meet the Emirati president during the Iran conflict, in what his office calls a historic breakthrough in relations between the two countries.

item.title: 💰 Fed officials signal rate hikes may be needed to curb inflation
item.description: Boston Fed President Collins and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari both indicated interest rates may need to rise if inflation pressures persist, with the Iran war worsening price pressures.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria urges global finance overhaul as debt costs consume half of revenue
item.description: President Tinubu called for reforming the global financial system, saying Nigeria will spend $11.6 billion on debt service in 2026 - nearly half of projected government revenue.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Bank of England to hold rates at 3.75% as Iran war fuels inflation
item.description: The BoE will likely keep rates steady this year, though a growing minority of economists expect a hike as energy price surges from the Iran conflict drive up inflation forecasts.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary summons Russian ambassador over drone strikes near border
item.description: Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador after drone strikes hit western Ukraine near the Hungarian border, in what PM Magyar called a violation of Hungarian security.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu held secret meeting with UAE president during Iran war
item.description: Israeli PM Netanyahu secretly visited the UAE to meet President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in March, discussing cooperation during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia launched covert attacks on Iran as regional war widened
item.description: Sources reveal Saudi Arabia conducted covert operations against Iranian targets as the US-Israel campaign expanded, marking a significant escalation in regional involvement.

item.title: 🏛️ US House members force vote on Ukraine aid despite leadership
item.description: A discharge petition reached 218 signatures, bypassing Speaker Johnson to bring Ukraine aid legislation to the House floor for a vote.

item.title: 💰 Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair amid rising inflation
item.description: The Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair, tasking him with navigating intensifying inflation pressures complicated by the Iran war.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran frees prominent rights lawyer Sotoudeh on bail
item.description: Human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh was released on bail after being arrested in April during Iran's crackdown amid the conflict with the US and Israel.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Nvidia CEO joins Trump's mission to open up China
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Elon Musk in his delegation, seeking to reopen Chinese markets to US technology companies.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia fires about 800 drones at Ukraine despite peace talks
item.description: Russia launched a massive drone barrage across 20 Ukrainian regions, killing at least six, even as Putin discussed potential peace with Trump.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine senator resists ICC arrest as gunshots heard at Senate
item.description: Gunshots rang out at the Philippine Senate as Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the ICC, urged supporters to resist arrest over drug war crimes.

item.title: ⛽ Sweden cuts fuel tax as part of $1.9 billion energy relief
item.description: Sweden announced fuel tax cuts and energy subsidies to help households cope with rising energy costs driven by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🏛️ House discharge petition reaches 218 signatures to force Ukraine aid vote
item.description: A discharge petition led by Rep. Kevin Kiley reached the threshold to bypass leadership and bring Ukraine aid to the House floor.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France probes alleged Israeli firm interference in local elections
item.description: French authorities are investigating whether an Israeli technology firm interfered in local elections amid broader concerns about foreign influence.

item.title: 🌏 US-China cooperation against scam centres in Dubai unprecedented
item.description: The US and China are cooperating on targeting scam centres in Dubai, an unusual instance of collaboration amid tensions.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia to join defensive Hormuz mission led by France and UK
item.description: Australia announced it will participate in a strictly defensive mission in the Strait of Hormuz, joining a European coalition amid Iran tensions.

item.title: 🇪🇹 Tigray tensions raise fears of Ethiopia-Eritrea war
item.description: Rising tensions in Tigray are stoking fears of renewed conflict between Ethiopia and Eritrea, threatening regional stability.

item.title: 🛡️ US special operations leaders frustrated by inability to modify equipment
item.description: Special operations commanders expressed frustration at bureaucratic hurdles preventing rapid modifications to their equipment.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel escalates Gaza attacks since halting Iran strikes, military says Hamas rearming
item.description: Israel has increased attacks on Gaza after pausing Iran operations, with the military saying Hamas fighters are regrouping and tightening control.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli strikes kill 12 in Lebanon ahead of US-mediated talks
item.description: Twelve people were killed by Israeli strikes on cars in Lebanon as conflict with Hezbollah continues before a third round of US-mediated talks.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China warns on US arms sales to Taiwan ahead of Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Beijing reiterated opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan before Trump's arrival, warning it is ready to crush any independence bid.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran war casts shadow over BRICS foreign ministers meeting in Delhi
item.description: The US-Israel Iran conflict will test BRICS unity at the two-day foreign ministers meeting in New Delhi starting Thursday.
